cubes.name: active_daily
cubes.title: Ballistar VN — Daily Active Snapshots (per user, per day)
cubes.description: One row per user per active day. Use this cube whenever you need a time window NOT pre-computed in mf_users (mf_users has rolling 30d only), OR to retrieve a user's day-by-day activity timeline for the 360 view. For best 1-user latency, ALWAYS pass a user_id equality filter.

measures.name: paying_dau
measures.description: Distinct active users with at least one recharge day in the selected window. Payer share / recharge rate = paying_dau / dau. Prefer this over grouping dau by is_recharge_day for multi-day windows — that split double-counts users who paid on some days but not others.

measures.name: mau
measures.description: Monthly active users — query with time_dimension granularity=month

measures.name: mau_prev_month
measures.description: Distinct users with at least one active day in the previous calendar month

measures.name: wau
measures.description: Weekly active users — unique users active in the ISO week (Mon-Sun); query with time_dimension granularity=week

measures.name: trailing_wau
measures.description: Trailing WAU — distinct active users from the start of the current ISO week (Monday) through today

measures.name: trailing_mau
measures.description: Trailing MAU — distinct active users from the start of the current calendar month through today

cubes.name: game_key_metrics
cubes.title: Ballistar VN — Game Key Metrics (daily, by acquisition slice)
cubes.description: Consolidated daily key-metrics mart, attributed to acquisition source (media_source / campaign / adset / ad / country / platform). Spend + installs + retention + revenue in one row. Use for ROAS/CAC, funnel, retention, and revenue reporting.

cubes.name: marketing_cost
cubes.title: Ballistar VN — Marketing Cost (all channels)
cubes.description: Daily ad spend by channel/campaign/adset/ad with impressions and clicks. Cost is provided in native currency plus normalized USD and VND.

measures.name: cost_native
measures.description: Spend in the campaign's native currency (see currency_code)

cubes.name: mf_users
cubes.title: Ballistar VN — User Master Profile
cubes.description: Wide feature store with one row per user. Pre-aggregates lifetime metrics (LTV, total active days, max role level) and rolling 30-day metrics (LTV 30d, recharge count 30d). Use this cube whenever a metric is already materialized here — Cube will read a single column instead of scanning event tables.

dimensions.name: install_date
dimensions.description: Attributed install date (from MMP). NULL for organic / pre-attribution users.

dimensions.name: country
dimensions.description: First-seen country code (ISO-2)

dimensions.name: total_active_days
dimensions.description: Lifetime distinct active days

dimensions.name: ltv_vnd
dimensions.description: Lifetime recharge value in VND

dimensions.name: ltv_30d_vnd
dimensions.description: Rolling 30-day recharge value (pre-aggregated in mf_users)

dimensions.name: is_paying_user
dimensions.description: Has any lifetime recharge

dimensions.name: lifecycle_stage
dimensions.description: Bucketed user lifecycle based on last activity recency

dimensions.name: churn_risk
dimensions.description: Churn-risk bucket for payers — at_risk = paying user inactive 7–30 days.

measures.name: user_count_approx
measures.description: Approx distinct via Trino approx_distinct (HLL) — fast, ~1.6% error

measures.name: ltv_30d_total_vnd
measures.description: Sum of pre-aggregated 30d LTV across users

measures.name: lapsed_this_month_count
measures.description: Approx count of users active last month but not yet this month

segments.name: vn_users
segments.description: Vietnam first-seen users

segments.name: whales
segments.description: Lifetime recharge >= 10M VND

segments.name: at_risk_paying
segments.description: Paying users inactive 7–30 days — churn risk

segments.name: lapsed_this_month
segments.description: Active in last calendar month but no login yet this month (last_active_date falls in previous month)

cubes.name: new_user_retention
cubes.title: Ballistar VN — New-User Retention Curves (daily cohorts)
cubes.description: NRU cohort retention by acquisition slice. rnru_NN = NRU still active N days after acquisition; ranpu_NN / rpnpu_NN = the payer-retention curves. Powers cohort retention charts directly from the consolidated mart.

cubes.name: ordered_event_funnel
cubes.title: ordered_event_funnel

dimensions.name: ts
dimensions.description: Event timestamp — REQUIRED filter. Always bound with a dateRange (max 31 days); unbounded queries are rejected before they full-scan the event tables.

cubes.name: ordered_funnel_canonical
cubes.title: Canonical Funnel (register → login → recharge, pre-aggregated)
cubes.description: Fixed-step first-occurrence funnel served from a pre-aggregation — use this for standard register/login/recharge funnel questions (fast, ~1-2% approximate counts). For custom step sets or step ordering, use ordered_event_funnel (live, exact, requires a bounded ts range).

dimensions.name: step_index
dimensions.description: Chronological position (1-based) of this event's first occurrence among the user's canonical events.

dimensions.name: step_name
dimensions.description: One of register / login / recharge.

dimensions.name: ts
dimensions.description: First occurrence timestamp of this event for this user (within the rolling 93-day window).

measures.name: step_count
measures.description: Approximate distinct users (HLL) whose first occurrence of this event sits at this step position.

cubes.name: recharge
cubes.title: Ballistar VN — Recharge Transactions (raw events)
cubes.description: One row per recharge transaction. Join key to mf_users is account_id (= user_id). For best 1-user latency, ALWAYS pass an account_id (or user_id via the joined view) equality filter.

dimensions.name: user_id
dimensions.description: User account id (= mf_users.user_id)

dimensions.name: value_vnd
dimensions.description: Charged value in VND (etl_ingame_recharge.charged_value)

dimensions.name: revenue_vnd
dimensions.description: Sum of all recharge value (VND) for this user on this day

measures.name: trailing_wpu
measures.description: Trailing WPU — distinct paying users from the start of the current ISO week (Monday) through today

measures.name: trailing_mpu
measures.description: Trailing MPU — distinct paying users from the start of the current calendar month through today

views.name: user_profile
views.description: Single-user wide profile (one row per user). Always filter by user_id with the equals operator for sub-second response.

views.name: user_activity_timeline
views.description: One row per day a user was active. Filter by user_id equals + date range and order by log_date desc for the activity-history panel of a 360 view.

views.name: user_recharge_timeline
views.description: One row per day a user recharged, with daily totals already pre-aggregated upstream. Faster than raw transactions — use this for the 360 revenue timeline. For per-transaction detail, see user_transactions.

views.name: user_transactions
views.description: One row per recharge transaction. Slower than user_recharge_timeline — only request when the 360 panel needs per-transaction detail.

views.name: user_audience
views.description: Segmentation surface — group/filter mf_users across cohorts. Use this for "how many VN whales are at risk", payer-tier breakdowns, etc. NOT for single-user lookups; see user_profile for that.

views.name: revenue_metrics
views.description: Daily / monthly revenue metrics from the recharge event stream.

views.name: activity_metrics

cubes.name: etl_login
cubes.title: CFM VN — Login (events, Login / 登录)
cubes.description: Per-role login events. Sourced from t-log `Login` (struct in docs/cfm_tlog_desc_20251210 (2).xml). This table carries `clientsdkuserid` (= mf_users.user_id, 19-digit), so it joins directly to cfm_mf_users without going through the role bridge. `roleid` is still exposed for in-game-character-level analysis. Profile signals here: hour/weekday distribution, device + network fingerprint per session, ladder score evolution, backflow markers. ALWAYS query with clientsdkuserid/user_id OR roleid filter + bounded log_date range, otherwise Trino will full-scan an event table.

dimensions.name: day_of_week_vn
dimensions.description: 1=Mon … 7=Sun in VN timezone

dimensions.name: system_hardware

dimensions.name: network

dimensions.description: PII — obs sidecar hashes filter values

cubes.name: etl_logout
cubes.title: CFM VN — Logout (events, Logout / 登出)
cubes.description: Per-role logout events. Sourced from t-log `Logout` (struct in docs/cfm_tlog_desc_20251210 (2).xml). This table carries `clientsdkuserid` (= mf_users.user_id, 19-digit), so it joins directly to cfm_mf_users without going through the role bridge. `roleid` is still exposed for in-game-character-level analysis. Pairs with cfm_etl_login: onlinetime here is per-session duration, so this cube gives session length / day rather than aggregate online time (which lives on cfm_active_daily). ALWAYS query with clientsdkuserid/user_id OR roleid + bounded log_date.

cubes.name: etl_lottery_shoot
cubes.title: CFM VN — Lottery / Gacha (events, LotteryShoot / 靶场抽奖)
cubes.description: Per-player gacha-pull events. Sourced from t-log `LotteryShoot` (struct in docs/cfm_tlog_desc_20251210 (2).xml). Joins via playerid → cfm_user_roles.role_id → cfm_mf_users.user_id. 213M rows — always filter by playerid or user_id + bounded log_date. LotteryId enum (per XML desc): 1=金币礼包, 2=金币礼包十连抽, 3=钻石礼包, 4=钻石礼包十连抽, 5=王者礼包, 6=王者礼包十连抽 (gold / diamond / king box, single + 10-pull variants). Result column = pull outcome.

dimensions.name: lottery_id
dimensions.description: Pool ID — 1/2 gold box, 3/4 diamond box, 5/6 king box (single/10-pull)

cubes.name: etl_money_flow
cubes.title: CFM VN — Money Flow (events, MoneyFlow / 货币流水)
cubes.description: Per-player in-game currency change events. Sourced from t-log `MoneyFlow` (struct in docs/cfm_tlog_desc_20251210 (2).xml). Joins via playerid → cfm_user_roles.role_id → cfm_mf_users.user_id. ⚠️ HUGE table — 1.35 BILLION rows. ALWAYS query with playerid OR user_id equality filter + bounded log_date range. Without filters, Trino will OOM. Pre-aggregations recommended before production use. Money type / reason enum labels in docs/CFL_Game detail 2.xlsx (Sheet1).

dimensions.name: log_date
dimensions.description: Partition column — REQUIRED filter for this table

dimensions.name: money_type_code
dimensions.description: Raw money type code — see docs/CFL_Game detail 2.xlsx Sheet1

dimensions.name: money_type
dimensions.description: VN label for in-game currency types. Authoritative source: docs/CFL_Game detail 2.xlsx Sheet1 (col A=label, col B=code). Verified against Trino distribution Apr-May 2026 — code 1 (Kim Cương) is the highest-volume currency, consistent with xlsx. Prior version had a wrong CASE — Kim Cương was mislabeled at code 0.

dimensions.name: reason_code
dimensions.description: Composite primary reason code (varchar). Encoded as reason_base + reason_action — divide by 1000 to get the currency-category base (Sheet1 col F/G), modulo 1000 to get the action (Sheet1 col J/L). Prefer the derived reason_base_label / reason_action_label dimensions below for human-readable analysis.

dimensions.name: reason_base_code
dimensions.description: Currency-category bucket (= reason DIV 1000 * 1000). See xlsx Sheet1 col F.

dimensions.name: reason_base_label
dimensions.description: VN label for the currency category implied by reason (Sheet1 col G).

dimensions.name: reason_action_code
dimensions.description: Specific action code (= reason MOD 1000). See xlsx Sheet1 col I.

dimensions.name: reason_action_label
dimensions.description: VN label for the specific reason action (Sheet1 col L).

dimensions.name: add_or_reduce
dimensions.description: '0' = increase, '1' = decrease

dimensions.name: i_money
dimensions.description: Amount of money the action involves

dimensions.name: delta
dimensions.description: Net change produced by this event

Unbridged transactions have no real user — their mf_users attributes are NULL; use the real_users_only segment to exclude them. NOTE: iamount is not the VND price — prefer user_recharge_daily for revenue reporting. For best 1-user latency, ALWAYS pass a vopenid equality filter.

dimensions.name: transaction_id
dimensions.description: Canonical VNG payment transaction id

dimensions.name: fsequence_no
dimensions.description: VNG SDK sequence number (alternative txn key)

dimensions.name: user_id
dimensions.description: VNG SDK openid — NOT the canonical mf_users.user_id (use gds_user_id for that)

dimensions.name: gds_user_id
dimensions.description: Canonical GDS user id, bridged from std_ingame_role_recharge by transaction. NULL for st_dummy test transactions (no real user).

dimensions.name: fuin
dimensions.description: Numeric VNG UID

dimensions.name: pay_channel_raw
dimensions.description: Raw SDK pay_channel (use payment_channel for canonical reporting)

dimensions.name: currency
dimensions.description: Currency code of charged_value (VND for VN users, may differ)

dimensions.name: offer_id
dimensions.description: VNG offer id (catalog of paid products)

dimensions.name: platid
dimensions.description: VNG platform id

dimensions.name: value_vnd
dimensions.description: Charged value (iamount). CAVEAT: not the actual VND price — use true_price_vnd (bridged from std) for real money amounts.

dimensions.name: true_price_vnd
dimensions.description: Actual VND price of the transaction, bridged from std_ingame_role_recharge.recharged_value. NULL for unbridged (no-real-user) transactions.

dimensions.name: value_source
dimensions.description: Charged value in original source currency

dimensions.name: value_usd
dimensions.description: CAVEAT: unit is US CENTS, not dollars (verified: 249,000 VND txn ↔ imoney_us 946.47 → $9.46 at ~26,300 VND/USD). Divide by 100 for USD.

dimensions.name: ingame_value
dimensions.description: In-game currency received

measures.name: revenue_vnd
measures.description: Sum of iamount. CAVEAT: iamount is not the actual VND price and raw totals are dominated by unbridged test traffic — prefer revenue_vnd_real (or user_recharge_daily) for money reporting.

measures.name: revenue_vnd_real
measures.description: Sum of actual VND prices (bridged from std_ingame_role_recharge). Unbridged test transactions contribute nothing (NULL), so this is the trustworthy transaction-grain revenue.

measures.name: revenue_usd
measures.description: CAVEAT: imoney_us is in US CENTS — this sum is 100x the dollar amount, and raw totals include unbridged test traffic. Prefer revenue_vnd_real (or user_recharge_daily) for money reporting.

segments.name: vnd_only
segments.description: Restrict to VND-charged transactions (drops foreign-currency edge cases)

segments.name: real_users_only
segments.description: Keep only transactions bridged to a canonical GDS user. Unbridged rows dominate raw iamount totals (~99% on sampled days) and look like load-test traffic with no real user behind them.

cubes.name: retention
cubes.title: retention

cubes.name: user_active_monthly
cubes.title: CFM VN — Monthly Active Snapshots (per user, per month)
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, log_month). Complements cfm_active_daily with a coarser, faster monthly rollup — use this for monthly retention curves, MAU trend, and the monthly band of the 1-user activity panel. For sub-daily detail, use cfm_active_daily instead. Source table column `ingame_first_active_month` is per-row (always equals log_month) — NOT lifetime. The lifetime first active month is derived here as `first_active_month_lt` via MIN() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id). `was_active_prev_month` is derived via LAG() over the same partition. These feed the `cohort_type` dimension (new / old / return).

dimensions.name: log_month
dimensions.description: First day of the month (DATE).

dimensions.name: log_year_month
dimensions.description: YYYY-MM string from the source. Prefer this dimension for monthly equality filters — sidesteps the Trino session-TZ skew that affects DATE/TIMESTAMP comparisons when Cube emits from_iso8601_timestamp.

dimensions.name: first_active_month
dimensions.description: Lifetime first active month (YYYY-MM) per user — derived via MIN(first_day_of_month) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id). Same value repeats on every row for a given user. Use as "register_month" for cohort classification.

dimensions.name: was_active_prev_month
dimensions.description: TRUE if the same user has a row for log_month - 1. Derived via LAG.

dimensions.name: cohort_type
dimensions.description: User-month cohort classification anchored on log_month: new = first_active_month_lt = log_month old = first_active_month_lt < log_month AND was_active_prev_month return = first_active_month_lt < (log_month - 1) AND NOT was_active_prev_month other = edge cases (e.g. registered prev month but no row prev month — shouldn't occur with consistent data)

cubes.name: user_active_rolling
cubes.title: CFM VN — Rolling Session Ratios (per user, as of the data anchor)
cubes.description: One row per user active in the trailing 30 days, evaluated as of the latest active date, carrying the session_ratio used by the session-drop care playbook.

dimensions.name: session_7d_avg
dimensions.description: Average daily online seconds over the trailing 7 calendar days

dimensions.name: session_30d_avg
dimensions.description: Average daily online seconds over the trailing 30 calendar days

dimensions.name: session_ratio
dimensions.description: 7d daily average online ÷ 30d daily average (raw; see qualified_session_ratio for the care signal)

dimensions.name: qualified_session_ratio
dimensions.description: session_ratio for heavy players still active (30d ≥ 30h, 7d > 0); NULL otherwise (<0.2 = sharp session-time drop)

cubes.name: user_devices
cubes.title: CFM VN — User ↔ Device Mapping
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, device_id). A user can play from many devices, a device can be used by many users — many-to-many bridge. Useful for "all devices used by user X" and shared-device detection. Always filter by user_id equality for 1-user 360 panels. NOTE: device_id is PII-class — surfaced behind public=false on views.

cubes.name: user_gameplay_daily
cubes.title: CFM VN — Daily Gameplay Standing (per user, as of the data anchor)
cubes.description: One row per user who played in the trailing 4 days, evaluated as of the latest match date, carrying global ladder rank, the 48h tier-drop, and the clan join/leave/switch signals used by the leaderboard and guild care playbooks. Identity: playerid → mf_ingame_roles.role_id → user_id.

dimensions.name: ladder_rank
dimensions.description: Global rank by current ladder score across all ranked players (1 = top)

dimensions.name: ladder_score
dimensions.description: This player's ladder score (totalladderscore) as of the anchor

dimensions.name: ladder_level
dimensions.description: This player's ladder tier (1-22) as of the anchor

dimensions.name: ladder_rank_drop_48h
dimensions.description: Ladder tiers dropped over the trailing 48h (positive = demoted); NULL if not observed both windows

dimensions.name: clan_id
dimensions.description: Current clan id ('0' = no clan)

dimensions.name: clan_changed_at
dimensions.description: Anchor date if the player switched clans in the trailing 48h, else NULL

dimensions.name: clan_left_at
dimensions.description: Anchor date if the player left their clan in the trailing 48h, else NULL

dimensions.name: clan_switched_recent
dimensions.description: 1 if the player switched between two real clans in the trailing 48h (guild instability)

dimensions.name: clan_left_recent
dimensions.description: 1 if the player left their clan (still active, now clanless) in the trailing 48h

cubes.name: user_ips
cubes.title: CFM VN — User ↔ IP Mapping
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, client_ip). Useful for fraud / shared-network detection and "all IPs seen for user X" panels in the 360 view. NOTE: client_ip is PII — surfaced behind public=false on views; the obs sidecar hashes filter values on this dimension automatically.

cubes.name: user_recharge_daily
cubes.title: CFM VN — Daily Recharge Snapshots (per user, per day)
cubes.description: One row per user per recharge day. Pre-aggregated upstream — much faster than scanning raw transactions for per-user timelines. For best 1-user latency, ALWAYS pass a user_id equality filter.

dimensions.name: revenue_vnd
dimensions.description: Sum of all recharge value (VND) for this user on this day

measures.name: trailing_wpu
measures.description: Trailing WPU — distinct paying users from the start of the current ISO week (Monday) through today

measures.name: trailing_mpu
measures.description: Trailing MPU — distinct paying users from the start of the current calendar month through today

cubes.name: user_recharge_monthly
cubes.title: CFM VN — Monthly Recharge Snapshots (per user, per month)
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, log_month) where the user recharged. Complements cfm_user_recharge_daily with a monthly rollup — use this for MRR trend, paying-user retention, and the monthly band of the 1-user revenue panel. For per-day detail use cfm_user_recharge_daily; per-transaction, cfm_recharge.

dimensions.name: revenue_vnd
dimensions.description: Total monthly recharge value (VND) for this user

measures.name: paying_users_monthly
measures.description: Distinct paying users in a given month (HLL approx)

cubes.name: user_recharge_rolling
cubes.title: CFM VN — Rolling Spend Ratios (per user, as of the data anchor)
cubes.description: One row per user who recharged in the trailing 30 days, evaluated as of the latest recharge date, carrying the spike/drop ratios used by the spend-spike and spend-drop care playbooks.

dimensions.name: revenue_1d
dimensions.description: This user's recharge value (VND) on the anchor day

dimensions.name: revenue_30d_avg
dimensions.description: Average daily spend over the trailing 30 calendar days (zero days included)

dimensions.name: spike_ratio
dimensions.description: anchor-day spend ÷ prior-29d daily average (≥3 = spend spike)

dimensions.name: drop_ratio
dimensions.description: 7d daily average ÷ 30d daily average (raw; see qualified_drop_ratio for the care signal)

dimensions.name: qualified_drop_ratio
dimensions.description: drop_ratio for engaged spenders still paying (30d ≥ ₫500k, 7d > 0); NULL otherwise (<0.3 = genuine spend drop worth a CS touch)

cubes.name: user_roles
cubes.title: CFM VN — Per-Role Master Profile (one row per role)
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, server_id, role_id). A single user can have many roles across servers. Use this cube for "show all characters of user X" and role-level high-water marks (level, fighting power, VIP). For 1-user latency, always pass a user_id equality filter. NOTE: per-role recharge columns (ingame_total_recharge_value_vnd*) are currently NOT populated upstream — measures/segments referencing them return 0. For revenue analytics, use cfm_user_recharge_daily / monthly / cfm_recharge instead. Kept here for forward-compat if ETL backfills.

dimensions.name: role_ltv_vnd
dimensions.description: Per-role lifetime recharge value (VND)

views.name: user_profile
views.description: Single-user wide profile (one row per user). Always filter by user_id with the equals operator for sub-second response.

views.name: user_activity_timeline
views.description: One row per day a user was active. Filter by user_id equals + date range and order by log_date desc for the activity-history panel of a 360 view.

views.name: user_recharge_timeline
views.description: One row per day a user recharged, with daily totals already pre-aggregated upstream. Faster than raw transactions — use this for the 360 revenue timeline. For per-transaction detail, see cfm_user_transactions.

views.name: user_transactions
views.description: One row per recharge transaction. Slower than cfm_user_recharge_timeline — only request when the 360 panel needs per-transaction detail.

views.name: user_audience
views.description: Segmentation + top-N surface (mf_users dims only — no event-stream joins). Two usage modes. (1) Cohort aggregation — group by cohort dimensions (country, payer_tier, lifecycle_stage, engagement_segment, ...) and read measures (user_count, paying_rate, ltv_total_vnd, ...). Do NOT include user_id. (2) Top-N user ranking — include user_id as a dimension plus any per-user numeric/time dimension (ltv_vnd, total_active_days, max_role_level, last_active_date, ...) and supply order = desc with a limit N for top-N. Use asc for bottom-N / oldest / least-active. Pair with segments (whales, vn_users, at_risk_paying, ...) to scope the leaderboard. For single-user profile lookup, use cfm_user_profile instead.

views.name: revenue_metrics
views.description: Daily / monthly revenue metrics from the recharge event stream.

views.name: activity_metrics
views.description: DAU / MAU / activity-day analytics from std_ingame_user_active_daily.

views.name: user_roles_panel
views.description: All characters/roles a user has played. Filter by user_id equals for the "characters" panel of a 360 view. Aggregates without a user filter give role-level cohort views (paying_roles, vip_roles, etc.).

views.name: user_devices_panel
views.description: All devices a user has played from. Useful for shared-device detection and the "devices" panel of a 360 view. device_id is PII — surfaced behind public=false on the underlying cube.

views.name: user_ips_panel
views.description: All IPs a user has connected from. Useful for fraud / shared-network analysis. client_ip is PII — surfaced behind public=false; obs sidecar hashes filter values automatically.

views.name: user_activity_monthly
views.description: Per-user, per-month activity rollup. Use for monthly retention curves, MAU trends, and the monthly band of the activity panel in a 360 view. For daily detail use cfm_user_activity_timeline.

views.name: user_revenue_monthly
views.description: Per-user, per-month revenue rollup. Use for MRR trend, paying-user retention, and the monthly band of the revenue panel in a 360 view. For daily detail use cfm_user_recharge_timeline; per-txn cfm_user_transactions.

views.name: user_matches_panel
views.description: Match-attempt events for one user. Filter by playerid (roles bridge) + log_date range. Aggregates without playerid give cohort-level match analytics (success rate, wait time by mode/map).

views.name: user_team_starts_panel
views.description: Team-match-start events for one user. Filter by playerid + log_date.

views.name: user_money_flow_panel
views.description: In-game currency change events. ⚠️ 1.35B-row source — ALWAYS filter by playerid + bounded log_date or query will OOM Trino.

views.name: user_lottery_panel
views.description: Gacha-pull events. Filter by playerid + log_date. 213M-row source.

views.name: user_tutorial_panel
views.description: Newbie-tutorial step events. Use for onboarding funnel + per-user tutorial-completion history. Filter by playerid + log_date.

views.name: user_newbie_detail_panel
views.description: Prologue / ship-training onboarding detail events. Filter by playerid.

views.name: user_login_panel
views.description: Per-session login events. Filter by clientsdkuserid (= user_id, direct join to mf_users) or roleid + bounded log_date. Powers the hour-of-day + weekday distribution + per-session device/network/ladder-score panels of a 360 view.

views.name: user_logout_panel
views.description: Per-session logout events. Filter by clientsdkuserid (= user_id, direct join to mf_users) or roleid + bounded log_date. Gives per-session duration (onlinetime) — for daily aggregate use cfm_user_activity_timeline.online_time_sec instead.

views.name: user_game_detail_panel
views.description: Per-match (per-round) PvP/PvE detail events. Filter by playerid + bounded log_date. Powers the PvP-mode breakdown, KDR, headshot-rate, accuracy, win-rate, and ladder-progression panels of a 360 view.

views.name: user_prop_flow_panel
views.description: Per-player item / weapon / skin transaction events. Filter by playerid + bounded log_date. Groups by reason / prop_type / direction reveal how the player acquires and spends in-game items.

cubes.name: active_daily
cubes.title: CROS — Daily Active Snapshots (per user, per day)
cubes.description: One row per user per active day. Use this cube whenever you need a time window NOT pre-computed in mf_users, OR to retrieve a user's day-by-day activity timeline for the 360 view. For best 1-user latency, ALWAYS pass a user_id equality filter.

measures.name: paying_dau
measures.description: Distinct active users with at least one recharge day in the selected window. Payer share / recharge rate = paying_dau / dau. Prefer this over grouping dau by is_recharge_day for multi-day windows — that split double-counts users who paid on some days but not others.

measures.name: mau
measures.description: Monthly active users — query with time_dimension granularity=month

cubes.name: etl_login
cubes.title: CROS — Login Events
cubes.description: Per-role login events. This table carries user_id (19-digit, = mf_users.user_id) directly, so it joins to cros_mf_users without a role bridge. role_id (9-char alphanumeric, e.g. BBBQL2232) is still exposed for in-game-character analysis. ALWAYS query with user_id OR role_id filter + bounded log_date range (queryRewrite enforces this).

dimensions.name: user_id
dimensions.description: SDK account user_id (19-digit) — direct join key to mf_users

dimensions.name: log_date
dimensions.description: Partition column — REQUIRED filter for this table

dimensions.name: hour_of_day_vn
dimensions.description: 0–23 in Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh

cubes.name: etl_logout
cubes.title: CROS — Logout Events
cubes.description: Per-role logout events with `online_time` (session duration in seconds). Joins directly to mf_users via user_id. ALWAYS query with user_id OR role_id filter + bounded log_date range (queryRewrite enforces this).

dimensions.name: log_date
dimensions.description: Partition column — REQUIRED filter

dimensions.name: online_time_sec
dimensions.description: Session duration in seconds (this logout event)

dimensions.name: session_band
dimensions.description: Bucketed online_time

cubes.name: etl_register
cubes.title: CROS — In-Game Register Events
cubes.description: Per-role first-character-create events. Carries user_id (= mf_users.user_id) so joins directly to mf_users. Use for time-to-create, channel/platform attribution of role creation, register-vs-install funnel. ALWAYS query with user_id OR role_id filter + bounded log_date range.

cubes.name: mf_users
cubes.title: CROS — User Master Profile
cubes.description: Wide feature store with one row per user. Pre-aggregates lifetime metrics (LTV, total active days, max role level) and rolling 30-day metrics (LTV 30d, recharge count 30d). Use this cube whenever a metric is already materialized here — Cube will read a single column instead of scanning event tables. CROS is a multi-region game (VN, ID, PH, SM, TH, TW + global); use country segments below to filter to a region.

dimensions.name: install_date
dimensions.description: Attributed install date (from MMP). NULL for organic / pre-attribution users.

dimensions.name: appsflyer_id
dimensions.description: AppsFlyer device-level attribution ID (one per install).

dimensions.name: country
dimensions.description: First-seen country code (ISO-2)

dimensions.name: total_active_days
dimensions.description: Lifetime distinct active days

dimensions.name: ltv_vnd
dimensions.description: Lifetime recharge value in VND

dimensions.name: ltv_30d_vnd
dimensions.description: Rolling 30-day recharge value (pre-aggregated in mf_users)

dimensions.name: lifecycle_stage
dimensions.description: Bucketed user lifecycle based on last activity recency

dimensions.name: engagement_segment
dimensions.description: Behavior-based engagement tier from lifetime ingame_total_active_days and engagement rate (active_days / days_since_install).

measures.name: user_count_approx
measures.description: Approx distinct via Trino approx_distinct (HLL) — fast, ~1.6% error

cubes.name: user_active_rolling
cubes.title: JUS VN — Rolling Session Ratios (per user, as of the data anchor)
cubes.description: One row per user active in the trailing 30 days, evaluated as of the latest active date, carrying the session_ratio used by the session-drop care playbook.

dimensions.name: session_7d_avg
dimensions.description: Average daily online seconds over the trailing 7 calendar days

dimensions.name: session_30d_avg
dimensions.description: Average daily online seconds over the trailing 30 calendar days

dimensions.name: session_ratio
dimensions.description: 7d daily average online ÷ 30d daily average (raw; see qualified_session_ratio for the care signal)

dimensions.name: qualified_session_ratio
dimensions.description: session_ratio for heavy players still active (30d ≥ 30h, 7d > 0); NULL otherwise (<0.2 = sharp session-time drop)

cubes.name: user_gameplay_daily
cubes.title: JUS VN — Progression Leaderboard (per user, role-level + LTV rank)
cubes.description: One row per progressed user (role_level > 0), ranked by max role level then lifetime recharge (LTV) as the tiebreak. ladder_rank = global progression rank (1 = most-advanced highest-value player) — jus's leaderboard, a cross-game reuse of the registry's user_gameplay_daily.ladder_rank member (which ranks by PvP ladder score on cfm; jus has no PvP ladder and no fighting-power data). Feeds the top-leaderboard and major-achievement care playbooks. No rank-drop / clan members (no jus source).

dimensions.name: role_level
dimensions.description: Max role level reached — primary ranking key

dimensions.name: ltv_vnd
dimensions.description: Lifetime recharge value (VND) — ranking tiebreak within a role level

dimensions.name: ladder_rank
dimensions.description: Global progression rank (1 = top). Ranked by role_level DESC, LTV DESC. RANK leaves gaps after ties.

cubes.name: user_recharge_daily
cubes.title: JUS VN — Daily Recharge Snapshots (per user, per day)
cubes.description: One row per user per recharge day. Pre-aggregated upstream — much faster than scanning raw transactions for per-user timelines. For best 1-user latency, ALWAYS pass a user_id equality filter.

dimensions.name: revenue_vnd
dimensions.description: Sum of all recharge value (VND) for this user on this day

measures.name: trailing_wpu
measures.description: Trailing WPU — distinct paying users from the start of the current ISO week (Monday) through today

measures.name: trailing_mpu
measures.description: Trailing MPU — distinct paying users from the start of the current calendar month through today

cubes.name: user_recharge_rolling
cubes.title: JUS VN — Rolling Spend Ratios (per user, as of the data anchor)
cubes.description: One row per user who recharged in the trailing 30 days, evaluated as of the latest recharge date, carrying the spike/drop ratios used by the spend-spike and spend-drop care playbooks.

dimensions.name: revenue_1d
dimensions.description: This user's recharge value (VND) on the anchor day

dimensions.name: revenue_30d_avg
dimensions.description: Average daily spend over the trailing 30 calendar days (zero days included)

dimensions.name: spike_ratio
dimensions.description: anchor-day spend ÷ prior-29d daily average (≥3 = spend spike)

dimensions.name: drop_ratio
dimensions.description: 7d daily average ÷ 30d daily average (raw; see qualified_drop_ratio for the care signal)

dimensions.name: qualified_drop_ratio
dimensions.description: drop_ratio for engaged spenders still paying (30d ≥ ₫500k, 7d > 0); NULL otherwise (<0.3 = genuine spend drop worth a CS touch)

cubes.name: user_roles
cubes.title: JUS VN — Per-Role Master Profile (one row per role)
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, server_id, role_id). A single user can have many roles across servers. Use this cube for "show all characters of user X" and role-level high-water marks (level, VIP). For 1-user latency, always pass a user_id equality filter. Primary use here: it supplies the in-game character name (last_role_name) that the VIP-care queue / member360 use as the player display name — without it the CS dashboard falls back to the raw uid. jus-specific NULLs (kept for forward-compat / cross-game member parity, mirroring cfm): fighting-power columns (战力 not collected in jus) and the per-role recharge columns (ingame_total_recharge_value_vnd*) are NOT populated upstream — measures/segments referencing them return 0. For revenue use user_recharge_daily / monthly / recharge instead.

dimensions.name: role_ltv_vnd
dimensions.description: Per-role lifetime recharge value (VND)

cubes.name: active_daily

measures.name: paying_dau
measures.description: Distinct active users with at least one recharge day in the selected window. Payer share / recharge rate = paying_dau / dau. Prefer this over grouping dau by is_recharge_day for multi-day windows — that split double-counts users who paid on some days but not others.

cubes.name: etl_login
cubes.title: TF — Login Events
cubes.description: Per-role login events (TGA event stream, ~18M rows). Keyed on role_id; joins to mf_users via the role bridge (role_id → tf_user_roles.role_id → user_id). `account_id` (12-digit) is the game-internal account, NOT mf_users.user_id. Event time is `utc_time` (UTC) + per-row `zone_offset` (hours). Football profile signals: lineup_rating, honor_point, vip_point, team composition. ALWAYS query with role_id filter + bounded log_date range (queryRewrite enforces a <= 31-day bound on this cube).

cubes.name: mf_users
cubes.title: TF — User Master Profile
cubes.description: Wide feature store with one row per user (1.99M users). Pre-aggregates lifetime metrics (LTV, total active days, max role level / fighting power) and rolling 30-day metrics (LTV 30d, recharge count 30d). Use this cube whenever a metric is already materialized here — Cube reads a single column instead of scanning event tables. TF is a multi-region SEA football team-management game (ID, TH, VN, MY, KH, LA, SG, PH); use country segments to scope a region. user_id is a 19-digit account id.

dimensions.name: install_date
dimensions.description: Attributed install date (from MMP). NULL for organic / pre-attribution users.

dimensions.name: country
dimensions.description: First-seen country code (ISO-2)

dimensions.name: total_active_days
dimensions.description: Lifetime distinct active days

dimensions.name: ltv_vnd
dimensions.description: Lifetime recharge value in VND (normalized across SEA currencies)

dimensions.name: lifecycle_stage
dimensions.description: Bucketed user lifecycle based on last activity recency

dimensions.name: engagement_segment
dimensions.description: Behavior-based engagement tier from lifetime ingame_total_active_days and engagement rate (active_days / days_since_install).

measures.name: user_count_approx
measures.description: Approx distinct via Trino approx_distinct (HLL) — fast, ~1.6% error

cubes.name: recharge
cubes.title: TF — Recharge Transactions (raw events)
cubes.description: One row per recharge transaction (TGA event stream). Keyed on role_id; joins to mf_users via the role bridge (role_id → tf_user_roles.role_id → user_id). `account_id` here is the game's internal account id (12-digit) — NOT mf_users.user_id, so it is not a join key. Currency is mixed across SEA (THB/VND/IDR/MYR/...) — `price`/`real_price`/`amount_rmb` are in the row's own `currency`; do NOT sum them across currencies. For VND-normalized revenue use tf_user_recharge_daily / tf_user_recharge_monthly instead. For best 1-user latency, pass a role_id equality filter.

dimensions.name: role_id
dimensions.description: In-game character id — bridge key to tf_user_roles / mf_users

dimensions.name: account_id
dimensions.description: Game-internal account id (12-digit) — NOT mf_users.user_id

dimensions.name: recharge_time
dimensions.description: Event time (UTC). Use zone_offset for local time.

dimensions.name: price
dimensions.description: Listed price in row currency (mixed — do not sum across currencies)

dimensions.name: real_price
dimensions.description: Actually-paid price in row currency

dimensions.name: amount_local
dimensions.description: Source column amount_rmb — observed equal to local price, not RMB-normalized

cubes.name: user_active_monthly
cubes.title: TF — Monthly Active Snapshots (per user, per month)
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, log_month). Complements tf_active_daily with a coarser, faster monthly rollup — use for monthly retention curves, MAU trend, and the monthly band of the 1-user activity panel. Source `ingame_first_active_month` is per-row (= log_month) — NOT lifetime. Lifetime first active month is derived here as `first_active_month_lt` via MIN() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id). `was_active_prev_month` via LAG(). These feed the `cohort_type` dimension (new / old / return).

dimensions.name: log_month
dimensions.description: First day of the month (DATE).

dimensions.name: log_year_month
dimensions.description: YYYY-MM string from the source. Prefer this for monthly equality filters — sidesteps the Trino session-TZ skew that affects DATE/TIMESTAMP comparisons when Cube emits from_iso8601_timestamp.

dimensions.name: first_active_month
dimensions.description: Lifetime first active month (YYYY-MM) per user — derived via MIN(first_day_of_month) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id).

dimensions.name: cohort_type
dimensions.description: User-month cohort classification anchored on log_month: new = first_active_month_lt = log_month old = first_active_month_lt < log_month AND was_active_prev_month return = first_active_month_lt < (log_month - 1) AND NOT was_active_prev_month other = edge cases

cubes.name: user_devices
cubes.title: TF — User ↔ Device Mapping
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, device_id). A user can play from many devices, a device can be used by many users — many-to-many bridge. Useful for "all devices used by user X" and shared-device detection. Always filter by user_id equality for 1-user 360 panels. NOTE: device_id is PII-class — public=false; obs sidecar hashes filter values.

cubes.name: user_ips
cubes.title: TF — User ↔ IP Mapping
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, client_ip). Useful for fraud / shared-network detection and "all IPs seen for user X" panels in the 360 view. NOTE: client_ip is PII — public=false; obs sidecar hashes filter values.

cubes.name: user_recharge_daily
cubes.title: TF — Daily Recharge Snapshots (per user, per day)
cubes.description: One row per user per recharge day. Pre-aggregated upstream with VND- normalized values (reliable revenue across SEA currencies) — much faster than scanning raw transactions. For best 1-user latency, ALWAYS pass a user_id equality filter.

dimensions.name: revenue_vnd
dimensions.description: Sum of all recharge value (VND) for this user on this day

cubes.name: user_recharge_monthly
cubes.title: TF — Monthly Recharge Snapshots (per user, per month)
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, log_month) where the user recharged. Complements tf_user_recharge_daily with a monthly rollup — use for MRR trend, paying-user retention, and the monthly band of the 1-user revenue panel.

cubes.name: user_roles
cubes.title: TF — Per-Role Master Profile (one row per role)
cubes.description: One row per (user_id, server_id, role_id). A single user can have many roles across servers. Use this cube for "show all characters of user X" and role-level high-water marks (level, fighting power, VIP). Also the bridge table that lets etl event cubes (keyed on role_id) reach mf_users: role_id → user_id → mf_users. For 1-user latency, always pass a user_id equality filter.

views.name: user_profile
views.description: Single-user wide profile (one row per user). Always filter by user_id with the equals operator for sub-second response.

views.name: user_activity_timeline
views.description: One row per day a user was active. Filter by user_id equals + date range and order by log_date desc for the activity-history panel of a 360 view.

views.name: user_recharge_timeline
views.description: One row per day a user recharged, with VND-normalized daily totals pre-aggregated upstream. Per-transaction detail in tf_user_transactions.

views.name: user_transactions
views.description: One row per recharge transaction (raw TGA events). role_id-keyed — filter by role_id (resolve from tf_user_roles_panel). Currency is mixed; use tf_user_recharge_timeline for VND revenue.

views.name: user_audience
views.description: Segmentation + top-N surface (mf_users dims only — no event-stream joins). (1) Cohort aggregation — group by cohort dims (country, payer_tier, lifecycle_stage, engagement_segment, ...) and read measures. Do NOT include user_id. (2) Top-N user ranking — include user_id plus a per-user numeric/time dimension and order desc/asc with a limit. Pair with segments (whales, vn_users, id_users, at_risk_paying, ...) to scope the leaderboard. For single-user profile lookup, use tf_user_profile.

views.name: revenue_metrics
views.description: Daily / monthly VND revenue metrics from std_ingame_user_recharge_daily (currency-normalized — preferred over raw recharge for revenue totals).

views.name: activity_metrics
views.description: DAU / MAU / activity-day analytics from std_ingame_user_active_daily.

views.name: user_roles_panel
views.description: All characters/roles a user has played. Filter by user_id equals for the "characters" panel — also the lookup that maps a user_id to its role_ids (needed to query the role-keyed event panels below).

views.name: user_devices_panel
views.description: All devices a user has played from. device_id is PII — public=false.

views.name: user_ips_panel
views.description: All IPs a user has connected from. client_ip is PII — public=false; obs sidecar hashes filter values automatically.

views.name: user_activity_monthly
views.description: Per-user, per-month activity rollup. For daily detail use tf_user_activity_timeline.

views.name: user_revenue_monthly
views.description: Per-user, per-month VND revenue rollup. For daily detail use tf_user_recharge_timeline.

views.name: user_login_panel
views.description: Per-session login events (role_id-keyed). Filter by role_id + bounded log_date. Powers session-frequency, device/network, and football progress (lineup_rating, honor_point) panels.

views.name: user_logout_panel
views.description: Per-session logout events with online_secs (session duration). role_id-keyed. Filter by role_id + bounded log_date.

views.name: user_register_panel
views.description: Per-role first-character-create events (role_id-keyed). Filter by role_id + bounded log_date. Use for time-to-create, channel/platform attribution, device profile at registration.
